hooks.prompt: mirdan quality context is active. For coding tasks: 1) Call mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt first for quality requirements and session context. 2) Call mcp__mirdan__get_quality_standards for the detected language. 3) Follow the quality_requirements from enhance_prompt output as constraints during implementation.

Code was just written or edited. Review the validation output above. If errors were found, fix them immediately. If the code touches auth, input handling, SQL, or API endpoints, call mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality with check_security=true for deeper analysis.

A tool call just failed. Before retrying: 1) Analyze the error — is it a permissions issue, invalid input, or a real bug? 2) If a Write/Edit failed, check if the file path is correct and the content is valid. 3) Consider an alternative approach rather than retrying the exact same call. 4) If validation tools failed, proceed with caution and validate manually before completing.

Before completing this task, verify quality was enforced: 1) Was mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt called at the start? If not, call it now. 2) Was mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality called on all changed files? If not, validate now. 3) Are there any unresolved errors from validation? If so, fix them. Do NOT complete the task if there are unresolved validation errors.

mirdan quality context is active. For any coding task: 1) Call mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt first for requirements. 2) After writing code, call mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality. 3) Fix all errors before considering the task complete.

Context is being compacted. CRITICAL: Preserve mirdan quality state in your compacted context using this exact format:
## mirdan Compacted State (Restore After Compaction)
- Session: <session_id from enhance_prompt>
- Task: <task_type>
- Language: <detected language>
- Security: sensitive|normal
- Last score: <most recent validation score>
- Open violations: <count of unresolved errors>
- Frameworks: <comma-separated list>
- Validated files: <list of files already validated>

After compaction, restore state by calling mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt with the preserved context to re-establish the quality session.
You are a subagent. mirdan quality standards are active. Validate any code you write with mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality before returning results.
Review the subagent's output for quality. If code was written, ensure it was validated with mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality.

Task completed. Final quality check: 1) List all files changed during this task. 2) Verify each was validated with mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality. 3) Confirm no unresolved errors remain. 4) If any file was missed, validate it now.

An agent teammate is idle. Consider assigning background quality work: 1) Identify files changed but not yet validated. 2) Run mcp__mirdan__validate_code_quality on unvalidated files. 3) Check for quality regressions across the session. 4) Report any new violations found.

A permission is being requested. Before granting: 1) Verify the action is necessary for the current task. 2) Check if the action could modify security-sensitive files (auth, secrets, configs). 3) Prefer minimal permissions — grant only what's needed. 4) If the action involves destructive operations (delete, overwrite), confirm intent.
Configuration was just changed. If .mirdan/config.yaml or quality-related settings were modified, call mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt to refresh quality context with the new configuration.

A new git worktree was created. Initialize mirdan quality context for this worktree: 1) Call mcp__mirdan__enhance_prompt to establish quality requirements for the worktree's task. 2) Note the worktree path for file validation.
A git worktree is being removed. Before cleanup: 1) Verify all code in the worktree was validated. 2) Note any unresolved quality issues for the main branch.
